revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=築山古墳| 所在地=岡山県瀬戸内市長船町西須恵| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀後半～6世紀前半| 規模=墳長82m
前方部幅66m
前方部高10m| 被葬者=| 史跡指定=岡山県史跡| 出土品=鏡、埴輪、玉類、武具、馬具| 画像=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]]|}} {{右| [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|家型石棺]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|須恵古代館]] |}} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''築山古墳'''（つきやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[長船町]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]である。 == 概要 == [[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半から[[6世紀]]前半に築造されたと考えられている。全長は約82[[メートル|m]]で、前方部は幅約66m・高さ約10m、後円部直径は約38m・高さ約9mと、後円部の方が前方部より低くなっている。北に延びた丘陵の端を加工して造営されている。墳丘は二段構造となっており各段の縁と墳丘の裾部に[[埴輪]]が配列されていた。 後円部の墳頂中央にあった竪穴式石室は割石小口積みで出来ていたが、[[明治]]40年（[[1907年]]）に発掘され墳頂部が削り取られた。このため家型石棺が露出している。家型石棺は[[凝灰岩]]製である。当初、[[大阪府]]と[[奈良県]]の県境にある[[二上山]]で産出されたものと推測されていたが、近年の研究では[[熊本県]][[宇土市]]産出の「[[阿蘇山|阿蘇]]ピンク石」であると考えられている。 [[昭和]]63年（[[1988年]]）に発掘調査が行われ、埴輪の配列のほか、墳丘には15cm大の角礫の葺き石が葺かれ、古墳の周囲には二重の周濠が巡らされていたことが確認されている。 出土品として、神人画像鏡1面・ガラス製勾玉・碧玉製管玉・鉄剣・鉄斧・直刀・槍・鉾・鉄鏃・短甲・冑・轡・杏葉などが出土し、現在これらは[[東京国立博物館]]に収蔵されている。 昭和34年（[[1959年]]）3月27日、岡山県の史跡に指定された。 ==須恵古代館== 古墳の北側に隣接し、高床式建物をイメージして[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）4月に開館した展示施設である。周辺の古墳や西谷遺跡、須恵器窯跡、廃寺などの遺跡から出土した陶器を中心に展示を行っている。開館日は土・日・祝日の9時30分より17時（但し入館は16時30分まで）。入館は無料。所在地は岡山県瀬戸内市長船町西須恵502-2。 ==参考文献== *乗岡実・行田裕美/編 近藤義郎/編集協力 『吉備の古墳 上〔備前・美作〕 -吉備考古ライブラリィ4-』 吉備人出版/刊 1996年 22-24ページ *岡山県高等学校教育研究会社会科部会歴史分科会/編 『新版 岡山県の歴史散歩』 山川出版社 1991年 47-48ページ *現地配布パンフレット『須恵古代館』 瀬戸内市教育委員会/発行 ==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきやまこふんせとうち}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:瀬戸内市]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=築山古墳| 所在地=岡山県瀬戸内市長船町西須恵| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀後半～6世紀前半| 規模=墳長82m
前方部幅66m

{{日本の古墳| 名称=築山古墳| 所在地=岡山県瀬戸内市長船町西須恵| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀後半～6世紀前半| 規模=墳長82m
前方部幅66m
前方部高10m| 被葬者=| 史跡指定=岡山県史跡| 出土品=鏡、埴輪、玉類、武具、馬具| 画像=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]]|}} {{右| [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|家型石棺]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|須恵古代館]] |}} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''築山古墳'''（つきやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[長船町]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]である。 == 概要 == [[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半から[[6世紀]]前半に築造されたと考えられている。全長は約82[[メートル|m]]で、前方部は幅約66m・高さ約10m、後円部直径は約38m・高さ約9mと、後円部の方が前方部より低くなっている。北に延びた丘陵の端を加工して造営されている。墳丘は二段構造となっており各段の縁と墳丘の裾部に[[埴輪]]が配列されていた。 後円部の墳頂中央にあった竪穴式石室は割石小口積みで出来ていたが、[[明治]]40年（[[1907年]]）に発掘され墳頂部が削り取られた。このため[[家型石棺]]が露出している。家型石棺は[[凝灰岩]]製である。当初、[[大阪府]]と[[奈良県]]の県境にある[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]で産出されたものと推測されていたが、近年の研究では[[熊本県]][[宇土市]]産出の「[[阿蘇山|阿蘇]]ピンク石」であると考えられている。 [[昭和]]63年（[[1988年]]）に発掘調査が行われ、埴輪の配列のほか、墳丘には15cm大の角礫の葺き石が葺かれ、古墳の周囲には二重の周濠が巡らされていたことが確認されている。 出土品として、神人画像鏡1面・ガラス製勾玉・碧玉製管玉・鉄剣・鉄斧・直刀・槍・鉾・鉄鏃・短甲・冑・轡・杏葉などが出土し、現在これらは[[東京国立博物館]]に収蔵されている。 昭和34年（[[1959年]]）3月27日、岡山県の史跡に指定された。 ==須恵古代館== 古墳の北側に隣接し、高床式建物をイメージして[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）4月に開館した展示施設である。周辺の古墳や西谷遺跡、須恵器窯跡、廃寺などの遺跡から出土した陶器を中心に展示を行っている。開館日は土・日・祝日の9時30分より17時（但し入館は16時30分まで）。入館は無料。所在地は岡山県瀬戸内市長船町西須恵502-2。 ==参考文献== *乗岡実・行田裕美/編 近藤義郎/編集協力 『吉備の古墳 上〔備前・美作〕 -吉備考古ライブラリィ4-』 吉備人出版/刊 1996年 22-24ページ *岡山県高等学校教育研究会社会科部会歴史分科会/編 『新版 岡山県の歴史散歩』 山川出版社 1991年 47-48ページ *現地配布パンフレット『須恵古代館』 瀬戸内市教育委員会/発行 *現地説明板 ==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきやまこふんせとうち}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:瀬戸内市]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=築山古墳| 所在地=岡山県瀬戸内市長船町西須恵| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀後半～6世紀前半| 規模=墳長82m
前方部幅66m
前方部高10m| 被葬者=| 史跡指定=岡山県史跡| 出土品=鏡、埴輪、玉類、武具、馬具| 画像=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]]|}} {{右| [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|家型石棺]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|須恵古代館]] |}} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''築山古墳'''（つきやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[長船町]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]である。 == 概要 == [[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半から[[6世紀]]前半に築造されたと考えられている。全長は約82[[メートル|m]]で、前方部は幅約66m・高さ約10m、後円部直径は約38m・高さ約9mと、後円部の方が前方部より低くなっている。北に延びた丘陵の端を加工して造営されている。墳丘は二段構造となっており各段の縁と墳丘の裾部に[[埴輪]]が配列されていた。 後円部の墳頂中央にあった竪穴式石室は割石小口積みで出来ていたが、[[明治]]40年（[[1907年]]）に発掘され墳頂部が削り取られた。このため[[家型石棺]]が露出している。家型石棺は[[凝灰岩]]製である。当初、[[大阪府]]と[[奈良県]]の県境にある[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]で産出されたものと推測されていたが、近年の研究では[[熊本県]][[宇土市]]産出の「[[阿蘇山|阿蘇]]ピンク石」であると考えられている。 [[昭和]]63年（[[1988年]]）に発掘調査が行われ、埴輪の配列のほか、墳丘には15cm大の角礫の葺き石が葺かれ、古墳の周囲には二重の周濠が巡らされていたことが確認されている。 出土品として、神人画像鏡1面・ガラス製勾玉・碧玉製管玉・鉄剣・鉄斧・直刀・槍・鉾・鉄鏃・短甲・冑・轡・杏葉などが出土し、現在これらは[[東京国立博物館]]に収蔵されている。 昭和34年（[[1959年]]）3月27日、岡山県の史跡に指定された。 ==須恵古代館== 古墳の北側に隣接し、高床式建物をイメージして[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）4月に開館した展示施設である。周辺の古墳や西谷遺跡、須恵器窯跡、廃寺などの遺跡から出土した陶器を中心に展示を行っている。開館日は土・日・祝日の9時30分より17時（但し入館は16時30分まで）。入館は無料。所在地は岡山県瀬戸内市長船町西須恵502-2。 ==参考文献== *乗岡実・行田裕美/編 近藤義郎/編集協力 『吉備の古墳 上〔備前・美作〕 -吉備考古ライブラリィ4-』 吉備人出版/刊 1996年 22-24ページ *岡山県高等学校教育研究会社会科部会歴史分科会/編 『新版 岡山県の歴史散歩』 山川出版社 1991年 47-48ページ *現地配布パンフレット『須恵古代館』 瀬戸内市教育委員会/発行 *現地説明板 ==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきやまこふん せとうち}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

前方部高10m| 被葬者=| 史跡指定=岡山県史跡| 出土品=鏡、埴輪、玉類、武具、馬具| 画像=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]]|}} {{右| [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|家型石棺]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|須恵古代館]] |}} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''築山古墳'''（つきやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[長船町]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]である。 == 概要 == [[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半から[[6世紀]]前半に築造されたと考えられている。全長は約82[[メートル|m]]で、前方部は幅約66m・高さ約10m、後円部直径は約38m・高さ約9mと、後円部の方が前方部より低くなっている。北に延びた丘陵の端を加工して造営されている。墳丘は二段構造となっており各段の縁と墳丘の裾部に[[埴輪]]が配列されていた。 後円部の墳頂中央にあった竪穴式石室は割石小口積みで出来ていたが、[[明治]]40年（[[1907年]]）に発掘され墳頂部が削り取られた。このため[[家型石棺]]が露出している。家型石棺は[[凝灰岩]]製である。当初、[[大阪府]]と[[奈良県]]の県境にある[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]で産出されたものと推測されていたが、近年の研究では[[熊本県]][[宇土市]]産出の「[[阿蘇ピンク石]]」であると考えられている。 [[昭和]]63年（[[1988年]]）に発掘調査が行われ、埴輪の配列のほか、墳丘には15cm大の角礫の葺き石が葺かれ、古墳の周囲には二重の周濠が巡らされていたことが確認されている。 出土品として、神人画像鏡1面・ガラス製勾玉・碧玉製管玉・鉄剣・鉄斧・直刀・槍・鉾・鉄鏃・短甲・冑・轡・杏葉などが出土し、現在これらは[[東京国立博物館]]に収蔵されている。 昭和34年（[[1959年]]）3月27日、岡山県の史跡に指定された。 ==須恵古代館== 古墳の北側に隣接し、高床式建物をイメージして[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）4月に開館した展示施設である。周辺の古墳や西谷遺跡、須恵器窯跡、廃寺などの遺跡から出土した陶器を中心に展示を行っている。開館日は土・日・祝日の9時30分より17時（但し入館は16時30分まで）。入館は無料。所在地は岡山県瀬戸内市長船町西須恵502-2。 ==参考文献== *乗岡実・行田裕美/編 近藤義郎/編集協力 『吉備の古墳 上〔備前・美作〕 -吉備考古ライブラリィ4-』 吉備人出版/刊 1996年 22-24ページ *岡山県高等学校教育研究会社会科部会歴史分科会/編 『新版 岡山県の歴史散歩』 山川出版社 1991年 47-48ページ *現地配布パンフレット『須恵古代館』 瀬戸内市教育委員会/発行 *現地説明板 ==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきやまこふん せとうち}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山県指定史跡]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)
[[Category:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]][[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]]

{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |Name=Tsukiyama Kofun |Location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |Coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |Image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Norioka Minoru and Yukimi Gyōda, eds., with editorial assistance by Yoshirō Kondō. *Kibi no Kofun, Part 1: Bizen and Mimasaka (Kibi Archaeological Library 4)*, Kibi Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Education Research Society History Division, eds. *New Edition: History Walk of Okayama Prefecture*, Yamato Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * Sue Kodai Museum Pamphlet, Setouchi City Board of Education. * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[ja:築山古墳]]

{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |Name=Tsukiyama Kofun |Location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |Coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |Image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Norioka Minoru and Yukimi Gyōda, eds., with editorial assistance by Yoshirō Kondō. *Kibi no Kofun, Part 1: Bizen and Mimasaka (Kibi Archaeological Library 4)*, Kibi Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Education Research Society History Division, eds. *New Edition: History Walk of Okayama Prefecture*, Yamato Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * Sue Kodai Museum Pamphlet, Setouchi City Board of Education. * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:築山古墳]]

These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Norioka Minoru and Yukimi Gyōda, eds., with editorial assistance by Yoshirō Kondō. *Kibi no Kofun, Part 1: Bizen and Mimasaka (Kibi Archaeological Library 4)*, Kibi Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Education Research Society History Division, eds. *New Edition: History Walk of Okayama Prefecture*, Yamato Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * Sue Kodai Museum Pamphlet, Setouchi City Board of Education. * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:築山古墳]]

{{Japanese burial mound| name=Tsukiyama Kofun| location=Setouchi City, Nagafune Town, Nishisue, Okayama Prefecture| coordinates={{ill|Coordinates|ja|座標}} {{ill|Latitude|ja|緯度}}: 34°40'46.5"N {{ill|Longitude|ja|経度}}: 134°8'14.4"E| shape=Keyhole-shaped burial mound| construction_period=Late 5th century – early 6th century| dimensions=Length: 82 m
Front width: 66 m
Front height: 10 m| buried_person=Unknown| historical_site_designation=Okayama Prefecture Historic Site| artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jade ornaments, weapons, equestrian equipment| image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]]|}} {{right| [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped stone coffin]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Ancient History Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Setouchi City|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. Its shape is that of a keyhole-shaped burial mound. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun is believed to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 5th century to early 6th century). The total length of the mound is approximately 82 meters, with the front portion measuring 66 meters wide and 10 meters high, and the rear circular section measuring 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The mound utilizes the end of a northern-extending ridge and features a two-tier structure. Arranged along the edges of the tiers and at the base of the mound were {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} clay figures. A vertically constructed burial chamber of stacked small stones once stood at the center of the rear mound but was excavated in {{ill|1907|ja|明治40年}}. This exposure left the house-shaped stone coffin visible. Initially, the coffin, made of tuff, was thought to originate from {{ill|Mt. Nijō|ja|二上山}}, straddling Osaka and Nara Prefectures. However, recent research suggests the material, referred to as "Aso Pink Stone," likely comes from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}} in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. Excavations in {{ill|1988|ja|昭和63年}} revealed an arrangement of haniwa and confirmed that the mound was covered with 15 cm angular gravel stones and surrounded by a double moat. Artifacts unearthed include a divine figure engraved mirror, glass magatama beads, jasper tubular beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, iron arrowheads, body armor, helmets, bridles, and decorative harness fittings. These items are now housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. On March 27, {{ill|1959|ja|昭和34年}}, the mound was designated as a historic site by Okayama Prefecture. == Sue Ancient History Museum == Adjacent to the north of Tsukiyama Kofun, the Sue Ancient History Museum opened in April {{ill|1996|ja|平成8年}}. Designed in the style of elevated buildings, the museum exhibits pottery and artifacts excavated from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue ware kilns, and ancient temples. The museum is open on weekends and public holidays from 9:30 am to 5:00 pm (last entry at 4:30 pm) and admission is free. It is located at Nishisue, Nagafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]][[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]]

{{Japanese burial mound| name=Tsukiyama Kofun| location=Setouchi City, Nagafune Town, Nishisue, Okayama Prefecture| coordinates={{ill|coordinates|ja|座標}} {{ill|Latitude|ja|緯度}}: 34°40'46.5"N {{ill|Longitude|ja|経度}}: 134°8'14.4"E| shape=Keyhole-shaped burial mound| construction_period=Late 5th century – early 6th century| dimensions=Length: 82 m
Front width: 66 m
Front height: 10 m| buried_person=Unknown| historical_site_designation=Okayama Prefecture Historic Site| artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jade ornaments, weapons, equestrian equipment| image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]]|}} {{right| [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped stone coffin]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Ancient History Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Setouchi City|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. Its shape is that of a keyhole-shaped burial mound. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun is believed to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 5th century to early 6th century). The total length of the mound is approximately 82 meters, with the front portion measuring 66 meters wide and 10 meters high, and the rear circular section measuring 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The mound utilizes the end of a northern-extending ridge and features a two-tier structure. Arranged along the edges of the tiers and at the base of the mound were {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} clay figures. A vertically constructed burial chamber of stacked small stones once stood at the center of the rear mound but was excavated in {{ill|1907|ja|明治40年}}. This exposure left the house-shaped stone coffin visible. Initially, the coffin, made of tuff, was thought to originate from {{ill|Mt. Nijō|ja|二上山}}, straddling Osaka and Nara Prefectures. However, recent research suggests the material, referred to as "Aso Pink Stone," likely comes from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}} in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. Excavations in {{ill|1988|ja|昭和63年}} revealed an arrangement of haniwa and confirmed that the mound was covered with 15 cm angular gravel stones and surrounded by a double moat. Artifacts unearthed include a divine figure engraved mirror, glass magatama beads, jasper tubular beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, iron arrowheads, body armor, helmets, bridles, and decorative harness fittings. These items are now housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. On March 27, {{ill|1959|ja|昭和34年}}, the mound was designated as a historic site by Okayama Prefecture. == Sue Ancient History Museum == Adjacent to the north of Tsukiyama Kofun, the Sue Ancient History Museum opened in April {{ill|1996|ja|平成8年}}. Designed in the style of elevated buildings, the museum exhibits pottery and artifacts excavated from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue ware kilns, and ancient temples. The museum is open on weekends and public holidays from 9:30 am to 5:00 pm (last entry at 4:30 pm) and admission is free. It is located at Nishisue, Nagafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[Category:Kk]][[category:new pages]][[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]]

{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |Name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Norioka Minoru and Yukimi Gyōda, eds., with editorial assistance by Yoshirō Kondō. *Kibi no Kofun, Part 1: Bizen and Mimasaka (Kibi Archaeological Library 4)*, Kibi Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Education Research Society History Division, eds. *New Edition: History Walk of Okayama Prefecture*, Yamato Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * Sue Kodai Museum Pamphlet, Setouchi City Board of Education. * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:築山古墳]]

The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Norioka Minoru and Yukimi Gyōda, eds., with editorial assistance by Yoshirō Kondō. *Kibi no Kofun, Part 1: Bizen and Mimasaka (Kibi Archaeological Library 4)*, Kibi Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Education Research Society History Division, eds. *New Edition: History Walk of Okayama Prefecture*, Yamato Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * Sue Kodai Museum Pamphlet, Setouchi City Board of Education. * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:築山古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Tsukiyama Kofun| location=Setouchi City, Nagafune Town, Nishisue, Okayama Prefecture| coordinates={{ill|coordinates|ja|座標}} {{ill|Latitude|ja|緯度}}: 34°40'46.5"N {{ill|Longitude|ja|経度}}: 134°8'14.4"E| shape=Keyhole-shaped burial mound| construction_period=Late 5th century – early 6th century| dimensions=Length: 82 m
Front width: 66 m
Front height: 10 m| buried_person=Unknown| historical_site_designation=Okayama Prefecture Historic Site| artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jade ornaments, weapons, equestrian equipment| image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]]|}} {{right| [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped stone coffin]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Ancient History Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Setouchi City|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. Its shape is that of a keyhole-shaped burial mound. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun is believed to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 5th century to early 6th century). The total length of the mound is approximately 82 meters, with the front portion measuring 66 meters wide and 10 meters high, and the rear circular section measuring 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The mound utilizes the end of a northern-extending ridge and features a two-tier structure. Arranged along the edges of the tiers and at the base of the mound were {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} clay figures. A vertically constructed burial chamber of stacked small stones once stood at the center of the rear mound but was excavated in {{ill|1907|ja|明治40年}}. This exposure left the house-shaped stone coffin visible. Initially, the coffin, made of tuff, was thought to originate from {{ill|Mt. Nijō|ja|二上山}}, straddling Osaka and Nara Prefectures. However, recent research suggests the material, referred to as "Aso Pink Stone," likely comes from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}} in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. Excavations in {{ill|1988|ja|昭和63年}} revealed an arrangement of haniwa and confirmed that the mound was covered with 15 cm angular gravel stones and surrounded by a double moat. Artifacts unearthed include a divine figure engraved mirror, glass magatama beads, jasper tubular beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, iron arrowheads, body armor, helmets, bridles, and decorative harness fittings. These items are now housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. On March 27, {{ill|1959|ja|昭和34年}}, the mound was designated as a historic site by Okayama Prefecture. == Sue Ancient History Museum == Adjacent to the north of Tsukiyama Kofun, the Sue Ancient History Museum opened in April {{ill|1996|ja|平成8年}}. Designed in the style of elevated buildings, the museum exhibits pottery and artifacts excavated from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue ware kilns, and ancient temples. The museum is open on weekends and public holidays from 9:30 am to 5:00 pm (last entry at 4:30 pm) and admission is free. It is located at Nishisue, Nagafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See also == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[Category:Kk]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]]

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Tsukiyama Kofun| location=Setouchi City, Nagafune Town, Nishisue, Okayama Prefecture| coordinates={{ill|coordinates|ja|座標}} {{ill|Latitude|ja|緯度}}: 34°40'46.5"N {{ill|Longitude|ja|経度}}: 134°8'14.4"E| shape=Keyhole-shaped burial mound| construction_period=Late 5th century – early 6th century| dimensions=Length: 82 m
Front width: 66 m
Front height: 10 m| buried_person=Unknown| historical_site_designation=Okayama Prefecture Historic Site| artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jade ornaments, weapons, equestrian equipment| image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]]|}} {{right| [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped stone coffin]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Ancient History Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Setouchi City|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. Its shape is that of a keyhole-shaped burial mound. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun is believed to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 5th century to early 6th century). The total length of the mound is approximately 82 meters, with the front portion measuring 66 meters wide and 10 meters high, and the rear circular section measuring 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The mound utilizes the end of a northern-extending ridge and features a two-tier structure. Arranged along the edges of the tiers and at the base of the mound were {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} clay figures. A vertically constructed burial chamber of stacked small stones once stood at the center of the rear mound but was excavated in {{ill|1907|ja|明治40年}}. This exposure left the house-shaped stone coffin visible. Initially, the coffin, made of tuff, was thought to originate from {{ill|Mt. Nijō|ja|二上山}}, straddling Osaka and Nara Prefectures. However, recent research suggests the material, referred to as "Aso Pink Stone," likely comes from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}} in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. Excavations in {{ill|1988|ja|昭和63年}} revealed an arrangement of haniwa and confirmed that the mound was covered with 15 cm angular gravel stones and surrounded by a double moat. Artifacts unearthed include a divine figure engraved mirror, glass magatama beads, jasper tubular beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, iron arrowheads, body armor, helmets, bridles, and decorative harness fittings. These items are now housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. On March 27, {{ill|1959|ja|昭和34年}}, the mound was designated as a historic site by Okayama Prefecture. == Sue Ancient History Museum == Adjacent to the north of Tsukiyama Kofun, the Sue Ancient History Museum opened in April {{ill|1996|ja|平成8年}}. Designed in the style of elevated buildings, the museum exhibits pottery and artifacts excavated from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue ware kilns, and ancient temples. The museum is open on weekends and public holidays from 9:30 am to 5:00 pm (last entry at 4:30 pm) and admission is free. It is located at Nishisue, Nagafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See also == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[Category:Kk]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]]

Front width: 66 m
Front height: 10 m| buried_person=Unknown| historical_site_designation=Okayama Prefecture Historic Site| artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jade ornaments, weapons, equestrian equipment| image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]]|}} {{right| [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped stone coffin]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Ancient History Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Setouchi City|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. Its shape is that of a keyhole-shaped burial mound. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun is believed to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 5th century to early 6th century). The total length of the mound is approximately 82 meters, with the front portion measuring 66 meters wide and 10 meters high, and the rear circular section measuring 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The mound utilizes the end of a northern-extending ridge and features a two-tier structure. Arranged along the edges of the tiers and at the base of the mound were {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} clay figures. A vertically constructed burial chamber of stacked small stones once stood at the center of the rear mound but was excavated in {{ill|1907|ja|明治40年}}. This exposure left the house-shaped stone coffin visible. Initially, the coffin, made of tuff, was thought to originate from {{ill|Mt. Nijō|ja|二上山}}, straddling Osaka and Nara Prefectures. However, recent research suggests the material, referred to as "Aso Pink Stone," likely comes from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}} in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. Excavations in {{ill|1988|ja|昭和63年}} revealed an arrangement of haniwa and confirmed that the mound was covered with 15 cm angular gravel stones and surrounded by a double moat. Artifacts unearthed include a divine figure engraved mirror, glass magatama beads, jasper tubular beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, iron arrowheads, body armor, helmets, bridles, and decorative harness fittings. These items are now housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. On March 27, {{ill|1959|ja|昭和34年}}, the mound was designated as a historic site by Okayama Prefecture. == Sue Ancient History Museum == Adjacent to the north of Tsukiyama Kofun, the Sue Ancient History Museum opened in April {{ill|1996|ja|平成8年}}. Designed in the style of elevated buildings, the museum exhibits pottery and artifacts excavated from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue ware kilns, and ancient temples. The museum is open on weekends and public holidays from 9:30 am to 5:00 pm (last entry at 4:30 pm) and admission is free. It is located at Nishisue, Nagafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See also == * {{ill|List of Infobox ancient site|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi City]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[Category:Kk]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]]

revision.text:
{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |Name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Norioka Minoru and Yukimi Gyōda, eds., with editorial assistance by Yoshirō Kondō. *Kibi no Kofun, Part 1: Bizen and Mimasaka (Kibi Archaeological Library 4)*, Kibi Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Education Research Society History Division, eds. *New Edition: History Walk of Okayama Prefecture*, Yamato Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * Sue Kodai Museum Pamphlet, Setouchi City Board of Education. * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:築山古墳]]

The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi)}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi)}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi)}}[[ja:築山古墳]]

It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi)}}[[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi)}}[[Category:dd]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi)}}[[ja:築山古墳]]

{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List
