Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Okayama revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Okayama revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Okayama {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Okayama {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of revision.text:
{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Okayama revision.text:
{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Okayama Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel.== See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Artifacts=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also! == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Okayama {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Construction_Period=Late 5th century to early 6th century |Scale=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Okayama It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of
