The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|12=draft}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市|12=draft}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[ja:%E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi)}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|12=draft}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市|12=draft}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[ja:%E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi)}} {{Kofun navbar}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|12=draft}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市|12=draft}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[ja:%E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi)}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|12=draft}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市|12=draft}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[ja:%E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] {{citation needed|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} {{citation needed|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi)}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|12=draft}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市|12=draft}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|12=draft}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市|12=draft}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi 8 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市) up to rev 82728376
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=築山古墳| 所在地=岡山県瀬戸内市長船町西須恵| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀後半～6世紀前半| 規模=墳長82m
前方部幅66m
前方部高10m| 被葬者=| 史跡指定=岡山県史跡| 出土品=鏡、埴輪、玉類、武具、馬具| 画像=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]]|}} {{右| [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|家型石棺]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|須恵古代館]] |}} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''築山古墳'''（つきやまこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[長船町]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]である。 == 概要 == [[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半から[[6世紀]]前半に築造されたと考えられている。全長は約82[[メートル|m]]で、前方部は幅約66m・高さ約10m、後円部直径は約38m・高さ約9mと、後円部の方が前方部より低くなっている。北に延びた丘陵の端を加工して造営されている。墳丘は二段構造となっており各段の縁と墳丘の裾部に[[埴輪]]が配列されていた。 後円部の墳頂中央にあった竪穴式石室は割石小口積みで出来ていたが、[[明治]]40年（[[1907年]]）に発掘され墳頂部が削り取られた。このため[[家型石棺]]が露出している。家型石棺は[[凝灰岩]]製である。当初、[[大阪府]]と[[奈良県]]の県境にある[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]で産出されたものと推測されていたが、近年の研究では[[熊本県]][[宇土市]]産出の「[[阿蘇ピンク石]]」であると考えられている。 [[昭和]]63年（[[1988年]]）に発掘調査が行われ、埴輪の配列のほか、墳丘には15cm大の角礫の葺き石が葺かれ、古墳の周囲には二重の周濠が巡らされていたことが確認されている。 出土品として、神人画像鏡1面・ガラス製勾玉・碧玉製管玉・鉄剣・鉄斧・直刀・槍・鉾・鉄鏃・短甲・冑・轡・杏葉などが出土し、現在これらは[[東京国立博物館]]に収蔵されている。 昭和34年（[[1959年]]）3月27日、岡山県の史跡に指定された。 ==須恵古代館== 古墳の北側に隣接し、高床式建物をイメージして[[平成]]8年（[[1996年]]）4月に開館した展示施設である。周辺の古墳や西谷遺跡、須恵器窯跡、廃寺などの遺跡から出土した陶器を中心に展示を行っている。開館日は土・日・祝日の9時30分より17時（但し入館は16時30分まで）。入館は無料。所在地は岡山県瀬戸内市長船町西須恵502-2。 ==参考文献== *乗岡実・行田裕美/編 近藤義郎/編集協力 『吉備の古墳 上〔備前・美作〕 -吉備考古ライブラリィ4-』 吉備人出版/刊 1996年 22-24ページ *岡山県高等学校教育研究会社会科部会歴史分科会/編 『新版 岡山県の歴史散歩』 山川出版社 1991年 47-48ページ *現地配布パンフレット『須恵古代館』 瀬戸内市教育委員会/発行 *現地説明板 ==関連項目== *[[日本の古墳一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきやまこふん せとうち}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山県指定史跡]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] to [[Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[ja:%E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]

Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[ja:%E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]

Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[ja:%E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setouchi, Okayama|qq=}}{{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osafune Town|qq=}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|qq=}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Osafune Town|ja|長船町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osafune Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setouchi, Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} to early {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tuff|qq=}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto City|ja|宇土市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto City|qq=}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[ja:%E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]

Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|qq=}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]], [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Okayama Prefecture. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[ja:%E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]

Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|qq=}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]], [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Okayama Prefecture. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[ja:%E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:岡山県の古墳]]

Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|qq=}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]], [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Okayama Prefecture. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[ja:%E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]]

Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|qq=}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]], [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Okayama Prefecture. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[ja:%E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]]

Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|qq=}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]], [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Okayama Prefecture. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[ja:%E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]]
