This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location=[[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]][[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]] |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|qq=}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]], [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Okayama Prefecture. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[sn:E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location=[[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]][[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]] |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|qq=|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]], [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Okayama Prefecture. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[sn:E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[Category:qq]]

Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[sn:E7%AF%89%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E7%80%AC%E6%88%B8%E5%86%85%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[sn:sukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]]

Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|qq=|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]], [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Okayama Prefecture. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}}

The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location=[[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]][[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]] |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]], [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Okayama Prefecture. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11603500]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location=[[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]][[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]] |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]], [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Okayama Prefecture. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11603500]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location=[[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]][[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]] |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]], [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Okayama Prefecture. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}}

Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]], [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Okayama Prefecture. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11603500]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location=[[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]][[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]] |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Osafune, Okayama|Osafune Town]], [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Okayama Prefecture. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] to early [[:en:6th century|6th century]]. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto City]], Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|en|Uto, Kumamoto|WD=Q861262|lt=Uto City}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} to early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|en|Uto, Kumamoto|WD=Q861262|lt=Uto City}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|en|Uto, Kumamoto|WD=Q861262|lt=Uto City}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)]][[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Tsukiyama Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|en|Setouchi, Okayama|WD=Q691691|lt=Setouchi, Okayama|1=Setouchi, Okayama}}{{ill|Osafune, Okayama|en|Osafune, Okayama|WD=Q7105505|lt=Osafune Town|1=Osafune, Okayama}} |coordinates={{Coord|34|40|46.5|N|134|8|14.4|E|}} |type={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |built=Late 5th century to early 6th century |map_size=Total length: 82m
Front section width: 66m
Front section height: 10m |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1959 |burial goods=Mirrors, haniwa, jewelry, weapons, horse gear |image=[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 02.jpg|260px]] }} {{Right |[[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 06.jpg|none|thumb|House-shaped sarcophagus]] [[File:Osafune Tsukiyama kofun 09.jpg|none|thumb|Sue Kodai Museum]] }} {{Commonscat|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Osafune, Okayama|en|Osafune, Okayama|WD=Q7105505|lt=Osafune Town|1=Osafune, Okayama}}, {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|en|Setouchi, Okayama|WD=Q691691|lt=Setouchi, Okayama|1=Setouchi, Okayama}}, Okayama Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}. == Overview == Tsukiyama Kofun was constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} to early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}. The tumulus measures approximately 82 meters in length, with the front section measuring 66 meters in width and 10 meters in height, and the rear circular section 38 meters in diameter and 9 meters in height. The tumulus was built by reshaping the tip of a hill extending to the north. The two-tiered mound features {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} arranged along its edges and base. A vertical stone chamber was located at the center of the rear circular section. Constructed with rubble masonry, it was excavated in 1907 (Meiji 40), exposing a house-shaped sarcophagus. The sarcophagus, made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}}, was initially believed to be sourced from {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}} on the Osaka-Nara border. However, recent studies indicate the material, known as "Aso Pink Stone," originates from {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|en|Uto, Kumamoto|WD=Q861262|lt=Uto City|1=Uto, Kumamoto}}, Kumamoto Prefecture. An excavation in 1988 (Shōwa 63) revealed the haniwa placement, paving stones of 15cm angular gravel, and a double moat surrounding the tumulus. Artifacts uncovered include a divine-human image mirror, glass magatama, jasper cylindrical beads, iron swords, axes, straight swords, spears, halberds, arrowheads, armor, helmets, and horse fittings such as bits and pendants. These items are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. The site was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture on March 27, 1959. == Sue Kodai Museum == Located adjacent to the tumulus's northern side, the Sue Kodai Museum opened in April 1996 (Heisei 8). Designed to resemble a raised-floor structure, the museum exhibits ceramics and artifacts from nearby kofun, the Nishitani archaeological site, Sue pottery kilns, and temple ruins. The museum is open on weekends and holidays from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM). Admission is free. Address: 502-2 Nishisue, Osafune Town, Setouchi City, Okayama Prefecture. == References == * Mino Norioka and Hiromi Gyōda (Eds.), Yoshio Kondo (Editor): ''Kibi Kofun Upper [Bizen, Mimasaka] – Kibi Archaeological Library 4,'' Kibito Publishing, 1996, pp. 22–24. * Okayama Prefectural High School Social Studies Division, History Division (Ed.): ''New Edition: History Walks in Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Publishing, 1991, pp. 47–48. * On-site pamphlet: ''Sue Ancient History Museum,'' Setouchi City Board of Education (Publisher). * On-site explanation panel. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|version=82728376|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)}} [[ja:築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11603502}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
